notes: Manual tracker dataset for the MV Hondius Andes virus outbreak. Latest headline count from WHO DON604 (data as of 27 May 2026): 13 total cases, including 11 laboratory-confirmed Andes virus infections and 2 probable cases, with 3 deaths (case fatality ratio 23%). Since the 13 May WHO update, three additional confirmed cases were reported (one each from Canada, the Netherlands, and Spain); a previously inconclusive U.S. case tested negative on 15 May and was removed from the count. More than 600 contacts (53% high-risk, 47% low-risk) are monitored across 32 countries, territories and areas. The effective reproduction number (Rt) was estimated at 0.7 as of 22 May 2026, indicating declining transmission. WHO recommends monitoring through 21 June 2026.

ship.name: MV Hondius
ship.lastKnownStatus: Following passenger disembarkation and repatriation, MV Hondius arrived in Rotterdam with 27 crew members on board.

route.event: Reported voyage departure point for Antarctic and Atlantic itinerary.
Earliest illness onset date reported in WHO outbreak notice.
Cluster of severe respiratory illness reported to WHO.
Medical evacuation of suspected and confirmed patients.

route.location: Port of Granadilla, Tenerife, Canary Islands
route.event: MV Hondius arrived for disembarkation and repatriation operations.
WHO stated the ship was on its way to the Netherlands after passengers left Tenerife.

passengerStatus.note: Case totals use WHO DON604 (data as of 27 May 2026): 13 total cases, 11 confirmed, 2 probable, 3 deaths. More than 600 contacts (53% high-risk, 47% low-risk) are monitored across 32 countries, territories and areas. Repatriated and monitoring numbers are tracker operational estimates from public WHO/ECDC updates.

timeline.title: Earliest reported illness onset
timeline.detail: WHO later reported illness onset among cases occurred between 6 and 28 April 2026.

timeline.title: WHO notified of cluster
timeline.detail: A cluster of severe respiratory illness aboard MV Hondius was reported to WHO.

timeline.title: WHO publishes first Disease Outbreak News
timeline.detail: WHO reported seven cases, including two laboratory-confirmed hantavirus cases and three deaths.

timeline.title: CDC issues MV Hondius statement
timeline.detail: CDC said the risk to the American public was extremely low and linked readers to hantavirus and Andes virus information.

timeline.title: WHO update identifies Andes virus
timeline.detail: WHO reported eight total cases, including six confirmed and two probable cases, with all confirmed cases identified as Andes virus.

timeline.title: MV Hondius arrives in Tenerife
timeline.detail: The ship arrived at the port of Granadilla, Tenerife, for disembarkation and repatriation.

timeline.title: WHO reports 11 cases and 3 deaths
timeline.detail: WHO reported 11 cases among passengers or crew: 9 confirmed Andes virus infections, 2 probable cases, and 3 deaths.

timeline.title: ECDC daily update keeps total at 11 cases
timeline.detail: ECDC reported 11 total cases: 8 confirmed, 2 probable, 1 inconclusive, 0 suspected, and 3 deaths, with no new cases or deaths since the previous update.

timeline.title: Australian passengers land for quarantine
timeline.detail: Australian CDC reported that six MV Hondius passengers landed at RAAF Base Pearce and moved to the Bullsbrook Centre for National Resilience; none had tested positive at the time of the update.

timeline.title: Canada reports one presumptive positive case
timeline.detail: PHAC reported one presumptive positive Andes virus case among returning Canadian passengers, with the traveller isolated in hospital and clinically stable.

timeline.title: Canada confirms the MV Hondius-linked case
timeline.detail: PHAC confirmed one Canadian MV Hondius passenger positive through laboratory testing; a travelling partner tested negative and no further Canadian cases had been identified.

timeline.title: ECDC keeps total at 12 cases
timeline.detail: ECDC reported 12 total cases: 9 confirmed, 2 probable, 1 inconclusive, 0 suspected, and 3 deaths, with no new deaths since the previous update.

timeline.title: MV Hondius arrives in Rotterdam
timeline.detail: ECDC reported that MV Hondius arrived in Rotterdam with 27 crew members on board after passenger disembarkation and repatriation.

timeline.title: WHO DON604: 13 cases, 3 deaths, Rt 0.7
timeline.detail: WHO published its fourth Disease Outbreak News. As of 27 May 2026, 13 total cases (11 laboratory-confirmed Andes virus, 2 probable) and 3 deaths (case fatality ratio 23%) were reported. Three additional confirmed cases since 13 May (one each from Canada, the Netherlands, and Spain, identified through contact follow-up and routine weekly testing); a previously inconclusive U.S. case tested negative on 15 May and was removed from the count. More than 600 contacts (53% high-risk) are monitored across 32 countries, territories and areas. The effective reproduction number was estimated at 0.7 as of 22 May, indicating declining transmission.
